mission.title: Install and Configure Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through the installation of the Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE), a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies cluster management on Vultr's infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a straightforward way to deploy and manage Kubernetes clusters without the overhead of manual setup. Key trade-offs include reliance on Vultr's infrastructure and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Vultr CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Vultr command-line interface, which is necessary for managing your Vultr resources.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/vultr/vultr-cli/releases/download/v1.0.0/vultr-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf vultr-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv vultr-cli /usr/local/bin/

vultr-cli version

```

> Download the latest Vultr CLI
> Extract the downloaded tarball
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH
> Verify the installation

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Vultr with the specified version and node count.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s create --region 'ewr' --version '1.30' --node-count 3 --plan 'vc2-2c-4gb'

```

> Replace 'ewr' with your desired region

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to allow kubectl to interact with your new cluster.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s kubeconfig --cluster-id <your-cluster-id> > ~/.kube/config

```

> Replace <your-cluster-id> with the actual cluster ID

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/vultr/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/monitoring/main/monitoring.yaml

```

> Deploy the cluster autoscaler
> Deploy monitoring tools

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s delete --cluster-id <your-cluster-id>

```

> Replace <your-cluster-id> with the actual cluster ID

uninstall.title: Remove Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent volumes are cleaned up.

```bash

vultr-cli loadbalancer list

vultr-cli loadbalancer delete --loadbalancer-id <lb-id>

```

> List load balancers to identify orphaned ones
> Replace <lb-id> with the actual load balancer ID

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This step upgrades your cluster to a new version.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s upgrade --cluster-id <your-cluster-id> --version '1.31'

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace <your-cluster-id> with the actual cluster ID
> Verify that nodes are upgraded and in 'Ready' state

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that your kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig file and ensure the cluster is running with 'vultr-cli k8s list'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient capacity in region'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are not enough resources available in the specified region.

**Fix:**
Try creating the cluster in a different region or reduce the node count.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the API key used does not have sufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your API key has the necessary permissions for Kubernetes operations.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Load balancer not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the load balancer service is not properly configured.

**Fix:**
Check the service configuration and ensure that the load balancer is created correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for automatic load balancer provisioning and improved node management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' with all nodes in 'Ready' state, indicating that your cluster is operational.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
